# Project: MiniVAgent

## Overview

MiniVAgent is a visual AI workflow tool — a node-based canvas where you connect nodes to build generative AI pipelines. Nodes can generate text, generate images, analyze images with vision models, or feed static content into the flow. The project runs locally and is not intended for public internet exposure.

![MiniVAgent Screenshot](docs/screenshots/mini-v-agent-v1.png)

## Architecture

Three-layer server stack:

```
Handler  (server/handlers/)  — HTTP only: parse request, call service, return JSON
Service  (server/services/)  — Business logic: typed options/results, calls AI APIs
Helper   (server/helpers/)   — Graph traversal, image saving, shared utilities
```

- **Handlers** never contain business logic.
- **Services** never import from `express`.
- **Helpers** are stateless utilities shared across services and handlers.

### Tech Stack

- **Backend:** Node.js, Express, TypeScript
- **Frontend:** React, TypeScript, Vite
- **AI Provider:** Google Gemini API (text, image, vision, planning)
- **Storage:** Local filesystem (graphs as JSON, resources as images)

## Project Structure

```
minivagent/
├── client/              # React frontend
│   ├── components/      # Canvas, node types, modals
│   └── services/        # API client wrappers
├── server/              # Express backend
│   ├── handlers/        # HTTP request handlers
│   ├── services/        # AI service integrations
│   ├── helpers/         # Graph traversal, execution
│   └── utils/           # Types, constants, utilities
├── data/
│   ├── graphs/          # Saved graph JSON files
│   └── resources/       # Uploaded image resources
├── docs/                # Documentation
└── tests/               # Vitest unit tests
```

## Node Types

| Type           | Purpose                        | Inputs              | Outputs            |
|----------------|--------------------------------|---------------------|--------------------|
| `TEXT_GEN`     | AI text generation             | `prompt`            | `prompt`, `output` |
| `IMAGE_GEN`    | AI image generation            | `prompt`, `image`   | `image-0`…`image-3`|
| `IMAGE_SOURCE` | Upload or URL image input      | —                   | `image`            |
| `IMAGE_TO_TEXT`| Vision / image analysis        | `prompt`, `image`   | `output`           |
| `NOTE`         | Static text / documentation    | —                   | `prompt`           |

## Graph Execution

Nodes execute in dependency order. Connected text and images are resolved by traversing the edge graph before each node runs.

**Prompt merging rules:**
- `TEXT_GEN`: `node.prompt + "\n\n--- Context ---\n" + connectedText`
- `IMAGE_GEN`: `node.prompt + " " + connectedText`

**Reference image resolution:**

| Format | Resolution |
|--------|-----------|
| `resource:<uuid>` | Read from `data/resources/` |
| `http://` / `https://` | Fetched at runtime |
| `data:…;base64,…` | Passed through unchanged |

**Node result shape** (same across `/run` and `/run/stream`):

```json
{
  "id": "node-id",
  "type": "TEXT_GEN",
  "data": { "prompt": "..." },
  "outputAssets": [
    { "type": "text", "value": "..." }
  ],
  "error": null
}
```

- `data` holds input/config: `{ prompt }` for TEXT_GEN, IMAGE_GEN, IMAGE_TO_TEXT, NOTE; `{}` otherwise
- `outputAssets` holds generated results:
  - Text: `{ type, value }`
  - Image: `{ type, mimeType, size, sizeStr, resourceId, url }`

**SSE events** (`/run/stream`):

| Event | Payload |
|-------|---------|
| `job_start` | `{ jobId, graphId, graphName, nodeCount, edgeCount, executionOrder }` |
| `node_start` | `{ nodeId, type }` |
| `node_end` | Full node result object |
| `job_done` | `{ status, graphId, graphName, nodeCount, edgeCount, executedNodes, elapsed, nodes, edges }` |
| `job_error` | `{ error }` |

## API Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/health` | Health check |
| GET | `/api/models` | All supported models by category |
| GET | `/api/meta` | All endpoints as JSON |
| GET | `/api/meta-llms` | All endpoints as Markdown (LLM-friendly) |
| POST | `/api/enhance-prompt` | AI-enhance a prompt |
| POST | `/api/generate-text` | Generate text from prompt |
| POST | `/api/extract-text-from-image` | Vision / image-to-text |
| POST | `/api/generate-images` | Generate images from prompt |
| POST | `/api/plan-graph` | Natural language → graph JSON |
| POST | `/api/render/run` | Execute an inline graph (nodes/edges in body, no saved file required) |
| POST | `/api/render/:graphId/run` | Execute saved graph, returns unified node states as JSON |
| POST | `/api/render/:graphId/run/stream` | Execute graph, streams results as SSE (`job_start`, `node_start`, `node_end`, `job_done`, `job_error`) |
| GET | `/api/graphs` | List saved graphs |
| GET | `/api/graphs/:graphId` | Get graph by ID |
| POST | `/api/graphs` | Create graph |
| PUT | `/api/graphs/:graphId` | Update graph |
| DELETE | `/api/graphs/:graphId` | Delete graph |
| GET | `/api/resources` | List image resources |
| POST | `/api/resources` | Upload image (`multipart/form-data`, field: `file`) |
| GET | `/api/resources/:id/info` | Resource metadata |
| GET | `/api/resources/:id` | Raw image binary |
| DELETE | `/api/resources/:id` | Delete resource |

## Setup & Commands

### Prerequisites

- Node.js v18+
- Google Gemini API key

### Install & Run

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Create .env.local with your API key
echo "GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here" > .env.local

# Development mode (hot reload, port 3202)
npm run dev

# Production build + serve (port 3201)
npm run dev:compiled
```

### Tests

```bash
npm test
npm run test:watch
npm run test:coverage
```

- [README.md](README.md): Full project overview and quick start
- [docs/node-types.md](docs/node-types.md): Node types reference
- [docs/supported-models.md](docs/supported-models.md): Supported AI models
- [docs/graph-id-conventions.md](docs/graph-id-conventions.md): Node/edge ID conventions
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Architecture rules and patterns for AI agents

## AI-Friendly

This project provides context for AI and LLM agents via [`llms.txt`](llms.txt), enabling its architecture and usage to be easily understood and indexed.
